data.body:
## Fix implemented + validated (experiment + OSS prior art)

**Change:** added `authorship.build_introducing_commit_map()` — one `git log --diff-filter=A --full-history --no-merges --no-renames --format=%x1e%H --name-only -- '*.json'` pass building a {path→oldest-add-commit} map. `verify_authorship.cli` builds it once; `_resolve_commit` looks each event up and falls back to the per-event `resolve_event_commit` only when the map lacks the path (fail-closed preserved).

**Experimental validation (real store, ~7.2k events / ~30k commits):**
- Correctness: 700 sampled events, batch map == per-event resolver, **0 mismatches, 0 misses**.
- Perf: per-event resolve ≈ 910 ms/event → **~1.8h** extrapolated full run (timed out at 280s before). Batched map builds in ~1.7s; **real gate end-to-end: 2.33s** (exit 0, 4031 report entries).

**OSS prior art (single-pass git-log + parse into a map is the established remedy for per-file `git log`):**
- Hugo `bep/gitmap` (`enableGitInfo`) — one `git log --name-only` pass, ASCII record-separator framing (the `%x1e` technique used here).
- Astro Starlight `getAllNewestCommitDate` — one `git log --name-status` pass into a Map.
- Docusaurus migrating away from per-file `getFileCommitDate` (issue #11208) to an eager whole-repo read; e18e ecosystem-issues #216 names per-file `git log` as the anti-pattern.
- Internal precedent: `build_dep_graph` single-`reduce_all_tickets`-scan refactor (tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py) — mirrored by the batch-invariant test here.

Refinements adopted from the research: `-c log.showSignature=false`, `--no-merges`/`--no-renames`, `--full-history`, oldest-wins overwrite. Tests: correctness parity, add-verification, batch-invariant (0 per-event calls), empty-map fallback, never-raises. lint + typecheck green.
